name: Documentary / Broadcast News
summary: Lower thirds, speaker ID cards, chapter markers, film grain. Fits video medium naturally

color_palette.primary: #F7F3EE (warm off-white background) or #0D1B2A (deep navy for broadcast dark mode)
color_palette.secondary: #1B263B (dark blue-gray for lower third bars), #E0D8CC (warm cream for light panels)
color_palette.accent: #B91C1C (red urgency bar - also Vietnam red), #D97706 (amber-gold for highlights/tickers)
color_palette.surface: #FFFFFF at 90-95% opacity for text backing panels
color_palette.text: #1C1917 (dark text on light), #F7F3EE (light text on dark lower thirds)
color_palette.notes: Broadcast design uses high-contrast solid blocks. Red bar + white text is a universal news visual language. Pairs naturally with Vietnamese red.

typography.font_families: Be Vietnam Pro (sans-serif) - excellent for broadcast clarity. Broadcast standard: clean sans-serif for lower thirds, heavier weight for names, lighter for titles/roles.
typography.size_scale: Speaker name: 40-48px bold, Speaker role/title: 28-32px regular, Chapter marker: 36-40px, Body text: 28-32px, Ticker/crawl: 24-28px
typography.vietnamese_diacritics_support: Excellent - Be Vietnam Pro handles broadcast-style bold weights with proper diacritic spacing. Sans-serif fonts are the broadcast industry standard precisely because they render cleanly at various sizes.

animation_style.approach: Slide-in from left/bottom for lower thirds (8-12 frames). Chapter markers wipe in. Film grain overlay at low opacity (3-5%). Spring config: damping 18-20, stiffness 120 (snappy, professional).
animation_style.entrance_patterns: Lower third slides in from left with slight overshoot. Name appears first, role slides in 5 frames later. Chapter markers use horizontal wipe. Content fades in above lower third.
animation_style.motion_budget: Low-moderate. Broadcast animations are quick and purposeful - no gratuitous motion. Film grain is a simple noise overlay (already have FilmGrain component). Most motion is entrance/exit of UI chrome.

projector_readability.contrast_ratio: Excellent. Broadcast design is built for worst-case viewing: small TVs, bright rooms, poor reception. Solid colored bars behind text guarantee contrast. No transparency ambiguity.
projector_readability.minimum_font_sizes: 32px+ for all broadcast UI elements. Lower thirds at 40px+ for names. This exceeds the 28px minimum easily.
projector_readability.color_washout_resistance: Very high. Solid color blocks (not gradients or transparency) maintain readability even on washed-out projectors. Red bar remains visible. White text on dark bar is projector-proof.

wcag_contrast_score.text_on_background: White #FFFFFF on dark bar #1B263B: 13.5:1 (AAA). Dark text #1C1917 on light background #F7F3EE: 14.8:1 (AAA). Red accent #B91C1C on white: 5.8:1 (AA large text).
wcag_contrast_score.recommendation: Broadcast design inherently achieves AAA contrast due to solid backing bars. No accessibility concerns.

projector_degradation_resistance.details: Broadcast design was literally engineered for degraded display conditions (analog TV, compression artifacts, bright environments). Solid color blocks, thick borders, high-contrast text survive projector color shift, brightness washout, and resolution loss. Film grain adds texture that masks projector artifacts.

vietnamese_diacritics_rendering.quality: Excellent. Lower third format gives each name generous vertical space. Bold sans-serif at 40px+ renders all Vietnamese diacritics perfectly. No clipping risk. Line-height 1.3-1.4 sufficient at broadcast sizes.

cultural_fit.assessment: Strong. Documentary format signals seriousness, research depth, and journalistic rigor - exactly what an academic presentation on Scientific Socialism should convey. Vietnamese state television (VTV) uses this exact broadcast aesthetic. Students will recognize it as 'professional' and 'official'.
cultural_fit.alignment_with_topic: Strong - documentary/broadcast format is how Vietnamese state media (VTV, QPVN) presents political and social content. Culturally appropriate for CNXHKH course.

cultural_semiotics.symbols_invoked: Authority, credibility, journalistic professionalism. Red accent bar = urgency/importance (also Vietnamese red). The broadcast format itself signals 'this is important information worth reporting.' Chapter markers echo VTV documentary style.
cultural_semiotics.risk: None - broadcast format is culturally neutral-to-positive for Vietnamese academic context

teacher_perception_signal.signal: Seriousness and professionalism. Documentary format communicates 'we treated this as real journalism/research, not a school project.' Shows understanding of video as a medium. Film grain adds production value. Teacher likely associates this with quality VTV documentaries.

rubric_alignment.format_15pct: Excellent - broadcast format is inherently well-structured with clear visual hierarchy. Lower thirds, chapter markers, and speaker IDs demonstrate professional video production understanding.
rubric_alignment.theory_35pct: Very good - documentary format supports extended text explanation with clean typography. Chapter markers create clear theoretical structure. Lower thirds identify sources/citations naturally.
rubric_alignment.application_50pct: Good - data can be presented in broadcast-style info panels and full-screen data cards. Ticker/crawl format can highlight statistics. However, less suited to complex data visualization than a dedicated infographic approach.

content_density_capacity.words_per_frame: 50-80 words. Documentary format supports dense information through layered presentation: main content area + lower third + optional ticker. Multiple information zones per frame are a broadcast convention.
content_density_capacity.data_viz_support: Good - broadcast uses full-screen data cards, side-by-side comparisons, and info panels. Not as flexible as pure infographic but very readable.

production_effort.assessment: Low-Medium. LowerThird component already exists. FilmGrain component exists. Need to add: chapter marker component, speaker ID card variant, transition wipes. Most work is styling existing components to broadcast conventions.
production_effort.migration_cost: Low - builds on existing LowerThird and FilmGrain. Add chapter markers and refine timing.

remotion_implementation_complexity.css_needs: Simple. Solid background bars, basic positioning (bottom 20% of frame), CSS transitions for slide-in. No backdrop-filter or complex effects.
remotion_implementation_complexity.threejs_interaction: 3D background can be retained but dimmed further. Broadcast style works best with simpler backgrounds (solid color or subtle gradient). Could replace 3D with film grain + solid color.
remotion_implementation_complexity.render_performance: Excellent. No GPU-intensive effects. Solid color blocks and text are the cheapest elements to render. Film grain is a simple noise overlay. Render time minimal.
remotion_implementation_complexity.computation_cost: Very low - simplest of all design options to render

distraction_level: Low - broadcast design is engineered to present information clearly without competing for attention. Lower thirds appear and disappear, leaving content in focus.
differentiation: Medium-High. Student presentations rarely adopt broadcast/documentary aesthetics. Most use PowerPoint templates or basic Canva designs. A broadcast-style video would stand out as professionally produced.

temporal_coherence.assessment: Excellent. Broadcast design systems are specifically built for long-form content (hours of news, 90-minute documentaries). Lower thirds, chapter markers, and consistent typography create strong identity over 13.5 minutes. Each speaker gets the same ID card treatment.
temporal_coherence.risk: Minimal. This style was designed for temporal coherence.

dark_vs_light_mode_viability: Highly flexible. Broadcast design works with both light and dark base themes. Lower thirds are self-contained solid blocks that read against any background. For projector: use light background with dark lower third bars.

examples: VTV (Vietnam Television) documentary programming - red/blue lower thirds with Vietnamese typography
BBC documentary style - clean lower thirds, chapter markers, authoritative presentation
Netflix documentary productions (e.g., 'The Social Dilemma') - modern broadcast design with motion graphics
CNN/Al Jazeera news design systems - structured information layers with clear hierarchy
Ken Burns documentaries - understated broadcast design that lets content lead
precedent.academic_context: Documentary format is commonly used for educational video content. Vietnamese state TV uses this format for political education programming (Phim tai lieu).
